---
title: "Bringing Work Trips Closer to Home"
subtitle: "WF-TDM Sketch-Level HBW Trip Redistribution Test"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    embed-resources: true
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    fig-align: center
    footer: "Wasatch Front Regional Council | Bringing Work Trips Closer to Home"
    include-before-body: ../../back_link.html
    include-after-body: ../../global_shift_toggle.html
    include-in-header: ../../slide_text_scale.html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: july2025
---

```{python}
#| label: setup

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import os
import sys
from IPython.display import HTML

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join('..', '..'))
from chart_utils import use_slide_chart_defaults, figure_with_shift_toggle, styled_table_with_toggle, CHART_CONFIG
use_slide_chart_defaults()

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join('..', '..', '..', 'run_sets', 'bring-work-trips-closer-to-home'))
import report_loader as loader

frames = loader.load()
scenario_ids = frames['scenario_ids']
corridor_volumes = frames['corridor_volumes']
freeway_by_county = frames['freeway_by_county']
corridor_orientation = frames['corridor_orientation']
vmt_vhd = frames['vmt_vhd_by_county_facility']
congested_miles = frames['congested_miles']
vht_hh = frames['vht_per_household']
transit = frames['transit_ridership']
trip_length = frames['hbw_trip_length']
trip_length_dist = frames['hbw_trip_length_distribution']
city_results = frames['city_results']

GEO_ORDER = loader.GEO_ORDER
GEO_COLORS = {'City Area': '#2a78d6', 'Medium District': '#1baf7a', 'Workshop Area': '#eda100'}
COUNTY_ORDER = ['Salt Lake', 'Davis', 'Utah', 'Weber', 'Box Elder', 'Region']
CITY_ORDER = sorted(loader.TARGET_CITIES.keys())
TRANSIT_LINE_ORDER = ['FrontRunner', 'TRAX Blue Line', 'TRAX Green Line', 'TRAX Red Line', loader.BUS_LABEL]
PERIOD_ORDER = loader.PERIOD_ORDER  # ['Peak', 'Off-Peak', 'Daily']

# Most charts below toggle between shift levels, and the VMT/VHD/VHT charts
# also toggle between Peak/Off-Peak/Daily -- both via chart_utils.
# figure_with_shift_toggle's button rows. A couple of fixed-number
# callouts/tables stay pinned at PRIMARY_SHIFT_PCT (and Peak, where
# relevant) since they're styled dataframes, not Plotly figures, and use
# their own Absolute/% Change toggle (styled_table_with_toggle) instead.
PRIMARY_SHIFT_PCT = 10

# Two-column slides (::: {.column width="50%"}) need each chart's own
# width override -- use_slide_chart_defaults()'s template default
# (SLIDE_CHART_WIDTH, full slide width) would otherwise overflow a 50%-wide
# column, since a Plotly figure's width is a fixed pixel size, not
# responsive to its container.
HALF_WIDTH = 540
```

## The Question

::: {.incremental}
- What if more people who live in an area also **worked** in that area — instead of commuting elsewhere?
- Sketch test: shift 5% / 10% / 25% of home-based-work trips leaving a target area back into it, following today's existing destination pattern
- Tested at three geography scales, each region-wide: **City Area** (lead, ~80 areas), Medium District (~48), Workshop Area (~12)
- 2027 RTP adoption-year network, model v10.0.0-beta.1
:::

::: {.notes}
This is a directional, order-of-magnitude check, not a land-use forecast — see the caveats slide.
:::

## Big Picture: All Shift Levels at Once {.smaller}

```{python}
SHIFT_COLORS = {'5%': '#a6cee3', '10%': '#3182bd', '25%': '#08306b'}

region_shift = vmt_vhd[
    (vmt_vhd['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vmt_vhd['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO) & (vmt_vhd['period'] == 'Peak')
].groupby(['geography_label', 'shift_pct'], as_index=False)[['delta_DY_VMT', 'base_DY_VMT', 'delta_DY_VHD', 'base_DY_VHD']].sum()
region_shift['pct_DY_VMT'] = region_shift['delta_DY_VMT'] / region_shift['base_DY_VMT'] * 100
region_shift['pct_DY_VHD'] = region_shift['delta_DY_VHD'] / region_shift['base_DY_VHD'] * 100
region_shift['shift_label'] = region_shift['shift_pct'].apply(lambda v: f'{int(v)}%')
shift_label_order = [f'{int(s)}%' for s in sorted(region_shift['shift_pct'].unique())]

def _big_picture(y_col, y_title, title):
    fig = px.bar(
        region_shift, x='geography_label', y=y_col, color='shift_label',
        barmode='group',
        category_orders={'geography_label': GEO_ORDER, 'shift_label': shift_label_order},
        color_discrete_map=SHIFT_COLORS,
        labels={'geography_label': '', y_col: y_title, 'shift_label': 'Shift'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.update_layout(height=420, width=HALF_WIDTH, title=dict(text=title, font_size=14))
    return fig
```

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{python}
_big_picture('pct_DY_VHD', 'Δ VHD (%)', 'VHD, All Shift Levels').show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{python}
_big_picture('pct_DY_VMT', 'Δ VMT (%)', 'VMT, All Shift Levels').show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```
:::
::::

Every shift level at once, region-wide, Peak period — no toggle needed to see how the effect scales with shift size.

## Congestion: Region-Wide VHD {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
vhd_region_df = vmt_vhd[(vmt_vhd['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vmt_vhd['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO)]
vhd_region_df = vhd_region_df.groupby(['geography_label', 'shift_pct', 'period'], as_index=False)[['delta_DY_VHD', 'base_DY_VHD']].sum()
vhd_region_df['pct_DY_VHD'] = vhd_region_df['delta_DY_VHD'] / vhd_region_df['base_DY_VHD'] * 100

def _build_vhd_region(sub, y_col='delta_DY_VHD'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='geography_label', y=y_col, color='geography_label',
        category_orders={'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
        labels={'geography_label': '', y_col: 'Δ VHD (hrs)'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    vhd_region_df, _build_vhd_region, pct_col='pct_DY_VHD',
    value_axis_title='Δ VHD (hrs)', pct_axis_title='Δ VHD (%)',
    period_col='period', default_period='Peak', period_order=PERIOD_ORDER,
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Change in Vehicle-Hours of Delay, Region', font_size=15))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

## VHD by County {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
vhd_df = vmt_vhd.copy()
vhd_df['CO_NAME'] = pd.Categorical(vhd_df['CO_NAME'], categories=COUNTY_ORDER, ordered=True)
vhd_by_county = vhd_df[vhd_df['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO].groupby(
    ['CO_NAME', 'geography_label', 'shift_pct', 'period'], as_index=False, observed=True
)[['delta_DY_VHD', 'base_DY_VHD']].sum()
vhd_by_county['pct_DY_VHD'] = vhd_by_county['delta_DY_VHD'] / vhd_by_county['base_DY_VHD'] * 100

def _build_vhd_county(sub, y_col='delta_DY_VHD'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='CO_NAME', y=y_col, color='geography_label', barmode='group',
        category_orders={'CO_NAME': COUNTY_ORDER, 'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
        labels={'CO_NAME': 'County', y_col: 'Δ VHD (hrs)', 'geography_label': 'Geography'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    vhd_by_county, _build_vhd_county, pct_col='pct_DY_VHD',
    value_axis_title='Δ VHD (hrs)', pct_axis_title='Δ VHD (%)',
    period_col='period', default_period='Peak', period_order=PERIOD_ORDER,
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Change in VHD by County', font_size=15))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

## Why: Vehicle Miles Traveled {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
vmt_by_county = vhd_df[vhd_df['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO].groupby(
    ['CO_NAME', 'geography_label', 'shift_pct', 'period'], as_index=False, observed=True
)[['delta_DY_VMT', 'base_DY_VMT']].sum()
vmt_by_county['pct_DY_VMT'] = vmt_by_county['delta_DY_VMT'] / vmt_by_county['base_DY_VMT'] * 100

def _build_vmt_county(sub, y_col='delta_DY_VMT'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='CO_NAME', y=y_col, color='geography_label', barmode='group',
        category_orders={'CO_NAME': COUNTY_ORDER, 'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
        labels={'CO_NAME': 'County', y_col: 'Δ VMT (mi)', 'geography_label': 'Geography'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    vmt_by_county, _build_vmt_county, pct_col='pct_DY_VMT',
    value_axis_title='Δ VMT (mi)', pct_axis_title='Δ VMT (%)',
    period_col='period', default_period='Peak', period_order=PERIOD_ORDER,
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Change in VMT by County', font_size=15))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

::: {.notes}
Shorter commutes mean less driving; less driving on congested facilities is why delay falls.
:::

## VMT vs. VHT/Household by City {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
scatter_df = city_results[city_results['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO].copy()
scatter_df['pct_VMT'] = scatter_df['delta_VMT'] / scatter_df['base_VMT'] * 100
scatter_df['pct_VHT_PER_HH'] = scatter_df['delta_VHT_PER_HH'] / scatter_df['base_VHT_PER_HH'] * 100
COUNTY_COLORS = {'Salt Lake': '#2a78d6', 'Davis': '#1baf7a', 'Utah': '#eda100', 'Weber': '#d6604d', 'Box Elder': '#8a6fbf'}
CITY_COUNTY_ORDER = sorted(scatter_df['CO_NAME'].unique(), key=lambda c: COUNTY_ORDER.index(c))

def _build_vmt_vs_vhd(sub):
    fig = px.scatter(
        sub, x='pct_VMT', y='pct_VHT_PER_HH', color='CO_NAME', text='city_label',
        category_orders={'CO_NAME': CITY_COUNTY_ORDER},
        color_discrete_map=COUNTY_COLORS,
        labels={'pct_VMT': 'Δ Daily HBW VMT (%)', 'pct_VHT_PER_HH': 'Δ Peak VHT/HH (%)', 'CO_NAME': 'County'},
    )
    fig.update_traces(marker=dict(size=13, line=dict(width=1, color='white')), textposition='top center')
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.add_vline(x=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(scatter_df, _build_vmt_vs_vhd, global_shift=True, numeric_x=True)

def _fixed(vals, pad_frac=0.10):
    lo, hi = vals.min(), vals.max()
    span = hi - lo
    pad = span * pad_frac if span else (abs(hi) * pad_frac or 1)
    return [lo - pad, hi + pad]

fig.update_xaxes(range=_fixed(scatter_df['pct_VMT']), tickformat='.1f', ticksuffix='%')
fig.update_yaxes(range=_fixed(scatter_df['pct_VHT_PER_HH']), tickformat='.1f', ticksuffix='%')
fig.update_layout(height=440, title=dict(text='Δ VMT vs. Δ VHT/HH, by City — City Area<br><sup>% of baseline</sup>', font_size=15))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

Lower-left quadrant (less VMT, less VHT/HH) is the expected pattern. City Area scenario only — VHT/HH is only computed per-city under this geography type.

## Severe Congestion: Miles Over Capacity {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
cm_ft = congested_miles[(congested_miles['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (congested_miles['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO)].copy()
cm_base_ft = cm_ft['base_congested_miles']
cm_ft['pct_congested_miles'] = (
    (cm_ft['delta_congested_miles'] / cm_base_ft.where(cm_base_ft > 0, 1) * 100).where(cm_base_ft > 0, 0.0)
)
cm_ft_order = cm_ft[cm_ft['period'] == 'Peak'].groupby('FTCLASS')['delta_congested_miles'].sum().sort_values().index.tolist()

def _build_congested_miles(sub, y_col='delta_congested_miles'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='FTCLASS', y=y_col, color='geography_label', barmode='group',
        category_orders={'FTCLASS': cm_ft_order, 'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
        labels={'FTCLASS': 'Facility Type', y_col: 'Δ Congested Miles', 'geography_label': 'Geography'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.update_layout(xaxis_tickangle=-15)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    cm_ft, _build_congested_miles, pct_col='pct_congested_miles',
    value_axis_title='Δ Congested Miles', pct_axis_title='Δ Congested Miles (%)',
    period_col='period', default_period='Peak', period_order=PERIOD_ORDER,
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Change in Miles of Severe Congestion (V/C &gt; 1.0)', font_size=15))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

Miles of roadway with volume/capacity > 1.0 — a length-based capacity read, distinct from delay (VHD), more directly comparable to how funding/capacity-expansion needs are scoped.

## Corridor Highlights — City Area {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
city_corr_primary = corridor_volumes[
    (corridor_volumes['geography_label'] == 'City Area') & (corridor_volumes['shift_pct'] == PRIMARY_SHIFT_PCT)
    & (corridor_volumes['period'] == 'Peak')
].sort_values('delta_DY_VHD')
top8_order = city_corr_primary['corridor_label'].head(8).tolist()

city_corr_all = corridor_volumes[
    (corridor_volumes['geography_label'] == 'City Area') & (corridor_volumes['corridor_label'].isin(top8_order))
].copy()
city_corr_all['corridor_label'] = pd.Categorical(city_corr_all['corridor_label'], categories=top8_order, ordered=True)
city_corr_all['pct_DY_VHD'] = city_corr_all['delta_DY_VHD'] / city_corr_all['base_DY_VHD'] * 100

def _build_top8(sub, y_col='delta_DY_VHD'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='corridor_label', y=y_col, color_discrete_sequence=[GEO_COLORS['City Area']],
        category_orders={'corridor_label': top8_order},
        labels={'corridor_label': 'Corridor', y_col: 'Δ VHD (hrs)'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.update_layout(xaxis_tickangle=-30)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    city_corr_all, _build_top8, pct_col='pct_DY_VHD',
    value_axis_title='Δ VHD (hrs)', pct_axis_title='Δ VHD (%)',
    period_col='period', default_period='Peak', period_order=PERIOD_ORDER,
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Top 8 Corridors by VHD Reduction — City Area', font_size=15))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

I-15, Redwood Road, Mountain View Corridor, and I-215 see the largest reductions (Peak period, ranking fixed at the primary shift level).

## Freeway Corridors by County {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
freeway_primary = city_corr_primary[city_corr_primary['corridor_label'].isin(loader.FREEWAY_CORRIDORS)]
freeway_order = freeway_primary.groupby('corridor_label')['delta_DY_VHD'].sum().sort_values().index.tolist()

freeway_city = freeway_by_county[freeway_by_county['geography_label'] == 'City Area'].copy()
freeway_city['corridor_label'] = pd.Categorical(freeway_city['corridor_label'], categories=freeway_order, ordered=True)
freeway_city['pct_DY_VHD'] = freeway_city['delta_DY_VHD'] / freeway_city['base_DY_VHD'] * 100

def _build_freeway_county(sub, y_col='delta_DY_VHD'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='corridor_label', y=y_col, color='CO_NAME', barmode='group',
        category_orders={'corridor_label': freeway_order, 'CO_NAME': COUNTY_ORDER},
        labels={'corridor_label': 'Freeway Corridor', y_col: 'Δ VHD (hrs)', 'CO_NAME': 'County'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.update_layout(xaxis_tickangle=-20)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    freeway_city, _build_freeway_county, pct_col='pct_DY_VHD',
    value_axis_title='Δ VHD (hrs)', pct_axis_title='Δ VHD (%)',
    period_col='period', default_period='Peak', period_order=PERIOD_ORDER,
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Freeway Corridor VHD Change by County — City Area', font_size=15))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

I-15's reduction concentrates in Salt Lake and Utah counties, where its own daily VHD is largest to begin with.

## Corridor Orientation: N/S vs. E/W {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
orientation_df = corridor_orientation[corridor_orientation['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO].copy()
orientation_df['pct_DY_VHD'] = orientation_df['delta_DY_VHD'] / orientation_df['base_DY_VHD'] * 100

def _build_orientation(sub, y_col='delta_DY_VHD'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='orientation', y=y_col, color='geography_label', barmode='group',
        category_orders={'orientation': ['N/S', 'E/W', 'Loop'], 'geography_label': GEO_ORDER},
        color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
        labels={'orientation': 'Corridor Orientation', y_col: 'Δ VHD (hrs)', 'geography_label': 'Geography'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    orientation_df, _build_orientation, pct_col='pct_DY_VHD',
    value_axis_title='Δ VHD (hrs)', pct_axis_title='Δ VHD (%)',
    period_col='period', default_period='Peak', period_order=PERIOD_ORDER,
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Change in VHD by Corridor Orientation', font_size=15))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

North/south corridors (I-15, Redwood Road, Mountain View Corridor, Bangerter Highway...) carry most of the reduction — the region's heaviest-volume facilities run north/south.

## Transit Ridership {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
transit_df = transit[transit['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO].copy()
transit_df['pct_Boardings'] = transit_df['delta_Boardings'] / transit_df['base_Boardings'] * 100

def _build_transit(sub, y_col='delta_Boardings'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='line_label', y=y_col, color='geography_label', barmode='group',
        category_orders={'line_label': TRANSIT_LINE_ORDER, 'geography_label': GEO_ORDER},
        color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
        labels={'line_label': 'Line', y_col: 'Δ Daily Boardings', 'geography_label': 'Geography'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    transit_df, _build_transit, pct_col='pct_Boardings',
    value_axis_title='Δ Daily Boardings', pct_axis_title='Δ Daily Boardings (%)',
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Change in Daily Boardings — Rail Lines &amp; Bus', font_size=15))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

"Bus" aggregates every non-rail route (~160 local/BRT/express codes). Shorter commutes mean fewer trips long enough for transit to compete on — ridership dips slightly on every line, including bus.

## Free Time &amp; Trip Length {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{python}
region_hh = vht_hh[(vht_hh['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vht_hh['geography_label'].isin(GEO_ORDER))].copy()
region_hh['delta_VHT_PER_HH_min'] = region_hh['delta_VHT_PER_HH'] * 60
region_hh['pct_VHT_PER_HH'] = region_hh['delta_VHT_PER_HH'] / region_hh['base_VHT_PER_HH'] * 100

def _build_vht_region(sub, y_col='delta_VHT_PER_HH_min'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='geography_label', y=y_col, color='geography_label',
        category_orders={'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
        labels={'geography_label': '', y_col: 'Δ VHT/HH (min)'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    region_hh, _build_vht_region, pct_col='pct_VHT_PER_HH',
    value_axis_title='Δ VHT/HH (min)', pct_axis_title='Δ VHT/HH (%)',
    period_col='period', default_period='Peak', period_order=PERIOD_ORDER,
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=420, width=HALF_WIDTH, title=dict(text='VHT / Household', font_size=14))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{python}
region_tl = trip_length[(trip_length['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (trip_length['geography_label'].isin(GEO_ORDER))].copy()
region_tl['pct_trip_length'] = region_tl['delta_trip_length'] / region_tl['base_trip_length'] * 100

def _build_tl_region(sub, y_col='delta_trip_length'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='geography_label', y=y_col, color='geography_label',
        category_orders={'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
        labels={'geography_label': '', y_col: 'Δ HBW Trip Length (mi)'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    region_tl, _build_tl_region, pct_col='pct_trip_length',
    value_axis_title='Δ HBW Trip Length (mi)', pct_axis_title='Δ HBW Trip Length (%)',
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=420, width=HALF_WIDTH, title=dict(text='Avg. HBW Trip Length', font_size=14))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```
:::
::::

## Trip Length Distribution {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
td = trip_length_dist.copy()

def _build_td(sub):
    fig = px.line(
        sub, x='bin_label', y='share_pct', color='geography_label', markers=True,
        category_orders={'bin_label': loader.DISTANCE_BIN_LABELS, 'geography_label': ['Baseline'] + GEO_ORDER},
        color_discrete_map={**GEO_COLORS, 'Baseline': '#898781'},
        labels={'bin_label': 'HBW Trip Length', 'share_pct': 'Share of Trips (%)', 'geography_label': 'Scenario'},
    )
    fig.update_traces(selector=dict(name='Baseline'), line=dict(color='black', dash='dash', width=2), marker=dict(color='black'))
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(td, _build_td, always_include=[0], period_col='period', default_period='Peak', period_order=PERIOD_ORDER, global_shift=True)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='HBW Trip Length Distribution, Baseline vs. Shift<br><sup>2-mile bins, Peak/Off-Peak-correct distance</sup>', font_size=15))
fig.update_xaxes(range=[-0.5, loader.DISTANCE_BIN_EDGES.index(30) - 0.5])
fig.update_yaxes(range=[0, 25])
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```

Shorter trips become more common, longer trips less common — the mechanism working as intended, region-wide.

## What This Means for a City {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{python}
city_df = city_results[city_results['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO].copy()
city_df['pct_trip_length'] = city_df['delta_trip_length'] / city_df['base_trip_length'] * 100
city_df['delta_VHT_PER_HH_min'] = city_df['delta_VHT_PER_HH'] * 60
city_df['pct_VHT_PER_HH'] = city_df['delta_VHT_PER_HH'] / city_df['base_VHT_PER_HH'] * 100

def _build_city_tl(sub, y_col='delta_trip_length'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='city_label', y=y_col, category_orders={'city_label': CITY_ORDER},
        color_discrete_sequence=[GEO_COLORS['City Area']],
        labels={'city_label': '', y_col: 'Δ Trip Length (mi)'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.update_layout(xaxis_tickangle=-20)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    city_df, _build_city_tl, pct_col='pct_trip_length',
    value_axis_title='Δ Trip Length (mi)', pct_axis_title='Δ Trip Length (%)',
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=420, width=HALF_WIDTH, title=dict(text='Δ HBW Trip Length by City', font_size=14))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{python}
def _build_city_vht(sub, y_col='delta_VHT_PER_HH_min'):
    fig = px.bar(
        sub, x='city_label', y=y_col, category_orders={'city_label': CITY_ORDER},
        color_discrete_sequence=[GEO_COLORS['City Area']],
        labels={'city_label': '', y_col: 'Δ Peak VHT/HH (min)'},
    )
    fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
    fig.update_layout(xaxis_tickangle=-20)
    return fig

fig = figure_with_shift_toggle(
    city_df, _build_city_vht, pct_col='pct_VHT_PER_HH',
    value_axis_title='Δ Peak VHT/HH (min)', pct_axis_title='Δ Peak VHT/HH (%)',
    global_shift=True,
)
fig.update_layout(height=420, width=HALF_WIDTH, title=dict(text='Δ Peak VHT/HH by City', font_size=14))
fig.show(config=CHART_CONFIG)
```
:::
::::

Ogden, Layton, Salt Lake City, West Jordan, Saratoga Springs, Roy, North Salt Lake, Mill Creek, Provo, Payson — City Area scenario. Saratoga Springs sees the largest change; Salt Lake City the smallest (already the region's largest local job base).

## Cross-Scale Comparison {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
vmt_vhd_primary = vmt_vhd[(vmt_vhd['shift_pct'] == PRIMARY_SHIFT_PCT) & (vmt_vhd['period'] == 'Peak')]
vht_hh_primary = vht_hh[(vht_hh['shift_pct'] == PRIMARY_SHIFT_PCT) & (vht_hh['period'] == 'Peak')]
trip_length_primary = trip_length[trip_length['shift_pct'] == PRIMARY_SHIFT_PCT]

abs_rows, pct_rows = [], []
for geo in GEO_ORDER:
    vhd_row = vmt_vhd_primary[(vmt_vhd_primary['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vmt_vhd_primary['geography_label'] == geo)]
    vhd, base_vhd = vhd_row['delta_DY_VHD'].sum(), vhd_row['base_DY_VHD'].sum()
    vmt, base_vmt = vhd_row['delta_DY_VMT'].sum(), vhd_row['base_DY_VMT'].sum()
    hh_row = vht_hh_primary[(vht_hh_primary['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vht_hh_primary['geography_label'] == geo)].iloc[0]
    hh_min, base_hh_min = hh_row['delta_VHT_PER_HH'] * 60, hh_row['base_VHT_PER_HH'] * 60
    tl_row = trip_length_primary[(trip_length_primary['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (trip_length_primary['geography_label'] == geo)].iloc[0]
    tl, base_tl = tl_row['delta_trip_length'], tl_row['base_trip_length']
    abs_rows.append({'Geography': geo, 'Δ VHD (hrs)': vhd, 'Δ VMT (mi)': vmt,
                      'Δ Peak VHT/HH (min)': hh_min, 'Δ Trip Length (mi)': tl})
    pct_rows.append({'Geography': geo, 'Δ VHD (%)': vhd / base_vhd * 100, 'Δ VMT (%)': vmt / base_vmt * 100,
                      'Δ Peak VHT/HH (%)': hh_min / base_hh_min * 100, 'Δ Trip Length (%)': tl / base_tl * 100})

cross_slide_style = [{'selector': 'th', 'props': [('background-color', '#2a78d6'), ('color', 'white')]}]
cross_abs_styler = pd.DataFrame(abs_rows).style.format({
    'Δ VHD (hrs)': '{:+,.0f}', 'Δ VMT (mi)': '{:+,.0f}', 'Δ Peak VHT/HH (min)': '{:+.2f}', 'Δ Trip Length (mi)': '{:+.2f}',
}).hide(axis='index').set_table_styles(cross_slide_style)
cross_pct_styler = pd.DataFrame(pct_rows).style.format({
    'Δ VHD (%)': '{:+.1f}%', 'Δ VMT (%)': '{:+.1f}%', 'Δ Peak VHT/HH (%)': '{:+.1f}%', 'Δ Trip Length (%)': '{:+.1f}%',
}).hide(axis='index').set_table_styles(cross_slide_style)
display(HTML(styled_table_with_toggle('tbl-cross-scale-slide', cross_abs_styler, cross_pct_styler)))
```

Fixed at the 10% shift level, Peak period (AM+PM). City Area and Medium District track closely; Workshop Area is consistently smaller — the coarsest geography, fewer areas region-wide.

## Caveats {.smaller}

:::  {.incremental}
- **Not a real land-use/economic forecast** — no businesses relocate, no zoning changes simulated
- **Feasibility not checked** — model doesn't confirm the area could hold the added jobs
- **Sketch-level, order-of-magnitude** — trips manually reallocated on today's existing pattern
- **HBW trips only** — other trip purposes untouched
- **No second-order effects** — housing costs, relocation, business response not captured
- **Same mechanism at all shift levels** — 5/10/25% test sensitivity to size, not to method
- **Corridor orientation/freeway groupings are a manual classification**, not per-segment geometry
:::

## Questions

**[Full write-up →](summary.html)**
